name: deezer-python

metadata.name: deezer-python
metadata.summary: A friendly wrapper library for the Deezer API

documentation:
# Deezer Python Client

--- **Documentation**: https://deezer-python.readthedocs.io **Source Code**: https://github.com/browniebroke/deezer-python --- A friendly Python wrapper around the [Deezer API](https://developers.deezer.com/api). ## Installation The package is published on [PyPI](https://pypi.org/project/deezer-python/) and can be installed by running: pip install deezer-python ## Basic Use Easily query the Deezer API from you Python code. The data returned by the Deezer API is mapped to python resources: ```pycon >>> client = deezer.Client() >>> client.get_album(680407).title 'Monkey Business' ``` Ready for more? Look at our whole [documentation](http://deezer-python.readthedocs.io/) on Read The Docs or have a play in pre-populated Jupyter notebook [on Binder](https://mybinder.org/v2/gh/browniebroke/deezer-python/main?filepath=demo.ipynb). ## Contributors

classes.name: deezer.resources.resource.Resource

methods.name: deezer.resources.resource.Resource.__repr__
documentation: Convenient representation giving a preview of the item.

methods.name: deezer.resources.resource.Resource.as_dict
documentation: Convert resource to dictionary.

methods.name: deezer.resources.resource.Resource.get_relation
documentation:
Generic method to load the relation from any resource.

Query the client with the object's known parameters
and try to retrieve the provided relation type. This
is not meant to be used directly by a client, it's more
a helper method for the child objects.

methods.name: deezer.resources.resource.Resource.post_relation
documentation:
Generic method to make a POST request to a relation from any resource.

This is not meant to be used directly by a client, it's more
a helper method for the child objects.

methods.name: deezer.resources.resource.Resource.delete_relation
documentation:
Generic method to make a DELETE request to a relation from any resource.

This is not meant to be used directly by a client, it's more
a helper method for the child objects.

methods.name: deezer.resources.resource.Resource.get_paginated_list
documentation: Build the pagination object based on the relation.

methods.name: deezer.resources.resource.Resource.__getattr__
documentation:
Called when the default attribute access fails with an AttributeError.

This is a fallback method, not need to call the parent implementation.
If the attribute is found through the normal mechanism, this is NOT called.

methods.name: deezer.resources.resource.Resource._infer_missing_field
documentation:
Hook to infer missing field values.

To be implemented in the subclasses for concrete resources.

methods.name: deezer.resources.resource.Resource.get
documentation: Get the resource from the API.

Base class for any resource.

It is mainly responsible for passing a reference to the client
to this class when instantiated, and transmit the json data into
attributes

documentation: Get an item or a slice of items from the list.

documentation: Iterate over the internal, fetching new pages as needed.

methods.name: deezer.pagination.PaginatedList.__len__
documentation: Get the total number of items across all pages.

methods.name: deezer.pagination.PaginatedList.total
documentation: The total number of items in the list, mirroring what Deezer returns.
Abstract paginated response from the API and make them more Pythonic.

classes.name: deezer.client.Client

methods.name: deezer.client.Client._process_json
documentation:
Recursively convert dictionary to {class}`~deezer.Resource` object.

```
param item

the JSON response as dict.

param parent

A reference to the parent resource, to avoid fetching again.

param resource_type

The resource class to use as top level.

param resource_id

The resource id to use as top level.

param paginate_list

Whether to wrap list into a pagination object.

returns

instance of 
```

methods.name: deezer.client.Client.request
documentation:
Make a request to the API and parse the response.

```
param method

HTTP verb to use: GET, POST< DELETE, ...

param path

The path to make the API call to (e.g. 'artist/1234').

param parent

A reference to the parent resource, to avoid fetching again.

param resource_type

The resource class to use as top level.

param resource_id

The resource id to use as top level.

param paginate_list

Whether to wrap list into a pagination object.
```

methods.name: deezer.client.Client.search
documentation:
Search tracks.

Advanced search is available by either formatting the query yourself or
by using the dedicated keywords arguments.

```
param query

the query to search for, this is directly passed as q query.

param strict

whether to disable fuzzy search and enable strict mode.

param ordering

see Deezer API docs for possible values.

param artist

parameter for the advanced search feature.

param album

parameter for the advanced search feature.

param track

parameter for the advanced search feature.

param label

parameter for the advanced search feature.

param dur_min

parameter for the advanced search feature.

param dur_max

parameter for the advanced search feature.

param bpm_min

parameter for the advanced search feature.

param bpm_max

parameter for the advanced search feature.

returns

a list of  instances.
```

methods.name: deezer.client.Client.search_albums
documentation:
Search albums matching the given query.

```
param query

the query to search for, this is directly passed as q query.

param strict

whether to disable fuzzy search and enable strict mode.

param ordering

see Deezer API docs for possible values.

return

list of  instances.
```

A client to retrieve some basic infos about Deezer resources.

Create a client instance with the given options. Options should
be passed in to the constructor as kwargs.

> ```pycon
> >>> import deezer
> >>> client = deezer.Client()
> ```

This client provides several methods to retrieve the content most
kinds of Deezer objects, based on their json structure.

Headers can be forced by using the `headers` kwarg.
For example, use `Accept-Language` header to force the output language.

> ```pycon
> >>> import deezer
> >>> client = deezer.Client(headers={'Accept-Language': 'fr'})
> ```

```
param access_token

user access token.

param headers

a dictionary of headers to be used.
```
